chunk_summary: A structured three-minute nutrition counselling script for outpatient visits, focusing on motivational interviewing techniques to address dietary habits and set SMART goals for behavior change.
learning_objectives: Understand the structure of a brief three-minute nutrition counselling session.
Learn to use motivational interviewing techniques, including reflective statements and readiness assessment.
Develop skills to create SMART goals and barrier plans for dietary behavior change.
Recognize when to pause counselling and address medical red flags.
key_facts: A three-minute intervention is feasible with a fixed structure and reflective statements.
Start with permission and agenda setting, e.g., 'Would it be okay to focus on one food habit?'
Advise with one targeted fact, e.g., 'Two cups of milk is enough; more can reduce appetite for meals.'
Assess readiness using a 0–10 scale and elicit strengths or barriers based on the response.
Convert choices into SMART goals and include a barrier plan, e.g., 'If grandparents offer biscuits, what could you offer instead?'
Medical red flags like weight loss, dehydration, or vomiting require pausing the script for stabilization or referral.
reference_quote: Motivational interviewing is a collaborative, goal-oriented style of communication with particular attention to the language of change. (Source: https://www.guilford.com/books/Motivational-Interviewing/Miller-Rollnick/9781609182274)
document_key_topics: SMART goal setting for dietary changes
Identifying and addressing medical red flags
